era_context: Continuation of the Iron Age II period. Shalmaneser III remains on the throne of Assyria, conducting his systematic campaigns of conquest and tribute collection. The Levantine kingdoms remain under Assyrian pressure. Greece continues in the Geometric period with increasing contact with Phoenician traders. Egypt remains fragmented.
geographic_coverage_gaps: East Asia (Chinese records exist but integration is incomplete)

events.title: Continuation of Shalmaneser III's Levantine campaigns
events.description: Shalmaneser III's military campaigns continued into the Levant as part of his systematic program of imperial expansion. The campaigns targeted the same coalition of Syrian and Palestinian states, extracting tribute and maintaining Assyrian hegemony. These campaigns are documented in royal inscriptions but lack year-specific detail for 813 BCE.
events.certainty_note: Shalmaneser III's reign is well-documented, and annual campaigns are attested. However, specific events of 813 BCE are not uniquely identified in surviving sources.

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: The historical record for 813 BCE is sparse and relies almost entirely on Assyrian royal inscriptions. These sources are propagandistic in nature and emphasize military victories and tribute collection. The perspectives of conquered peoples, non-elite populations, and regions outside the Assyrian sphere are absent from the surviving record.

graph_edges.note: Part of the same campaign cycle as 814 BCE
